zh.lifeSummary: 當Oliver de La Zouche於1250年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾阿什比 德 拉 祖時，他的父親Alan la Zouche I I45歲，母親Helen Quincy28歲。他於大約1279年與Lady Iseult de Chernet在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾素享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Georges, Adrien.
from Old French bosche ‘large bundle of firewood’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a seller of such bundles.

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Oliver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a French name, Olivier, recorded as the name of one of Charlemagne's paladins (retainers), the close companion in arms of Roland in the Chanson de Roland. Whereas Roland is headstrong and rash, Oliver is thoughtful and cautious. Ostensibly this name derives from Late Latin olivarius ‘olive tree’ ( compare Olive ), but Charlemagne's other paladins all bear solidly Germanic names, so it is more probably an altered form of a Germanic name, perhaps distantly connected with Old Norse Óleifr ‘ancestral relic’. It has remained in more or less continuous use since the medieval period, becoming ever more popular since the 1980s.

en.lifeSummary: When Oliver de La Zouche was born in 1250, in Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alan la Zouche I I, was 45 and his mother, Helen Quincy, was 28. He married Lady Iseult de Chernet about 1279, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1328, in Southoe, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

Il a épousé Lady Iseult de Chernet environ 1279, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1328, à Southoe, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 78 ans.

ko.Ashby, Leicestershire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치
ko.lifeSummary: Oliver de La Zouche(이)가 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan la Zouche I I(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Helen Quincy(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1279년 영국에서 Lady Iseult de Chernet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 소우토에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Ashby, Leicestershire, England: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Oliver de La Zouche родился в 1250 г. в местности ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alan la Zouche I I было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen Quincy, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Iseult de Chernet около 1279 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1328 г. в местности соуто, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 78 лет.
